channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Food Prices Drop, But Inflation Risks Rise as Energy Costs Increase
item.description: Food prices in the UK have fallen at their slowest rate in nearly two years, with staples like margarine and sugar seeing price declines due to supermarket competition and summer sales. However, overa...

item.title: Southern California Faces Severe Heatwave as Temperatures Rise
item.description: A prolonged heatwave is gripping Southern California, with temperatures reaching record highs and posing significant risks to public health, infrastructure, and ecosystems. The National Weather Servic...

item.title: Junior Springboks Doctor Saves Passenger's Life Mid-Flight
item.description: Dr. Clement Plaatjies, the team doctor of the Junior Springboks, recently demonstrated exceptional professionalism by saving a passenger's life during a flight from Johannesburg to Cape Town. The inci...

item.title: Post-Tariff Expiry: India-US Trade Outlook and Potential Tariff Changes
item.description: The 10% tariff imposed by the Donald Trump administration on most Indian goods to the US, which has been in effect since February 2026, is set to expire on July 24. This temporary measure was a stop-g...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari Discusses Decision to Remove Phones from Children
item.description: Pastor Kanyari has shared why his children have stepped back from social media, revealing he reclaimed the phones he gifted them to limit their online engagement. During a church service, he explained...

item.title: UK Inflation Drops as Burnham Focuses on Cost-of-Living Measures
item.description: The UK's inflation rate dropped to 2.6% in June, down from 2.8% in May, according to official data. This decline is mainly due to lower fuel prices after the US-Iran ceasefire, which reduced crude oil...

item.title: Tanzania introduces one-year internship for new teachers before licensing
item.description: Tanzania has implemented a new policy requiring education graduates to complete a one-year professional internship before obtaining teaching licenses. Education Commissioner Dr Lyabwene Mtahabwa confi...

item.title: Wanderers Defy Tigers in Australia Cup Clash as Flares Spark Chaos at McKellar Park
item.description: The Western Sydney Wanderers have dismissed claims of a 'cupset' after their narrow victory over Tigers FC in the Australia Cup round of 32 at McKellar Park. The match, which saw flares ignited by fan...

item.title: Trump Faces Criticism for Controversial Defense of Iran War Escalation
item.description: The article details President Donald Trump's controversial defense of his Iran war escalation, which drew sharp criticism. During a segment on MS NOW, host Stephanie Ruhle expressed dismay after Trump...

item.title: Philippines Condemns China's Violent Incursion into Ayungin Shoal, Asserts Sovereignty
item.description: The Municipality of Kalayaan in the West Philippine Sea condemned China's recent violent confrontation with Philippine Navy personnel at Ayungin Shoal, accusing Beijing of operating under an 'imperial...

item.title: Social Media Risks for Tanzanian Children: Parental Guidance Needed
item.description: The article highlights the growing concerns about children's exposure to online risks through social media platforms. With 67% of Tanzanian children aged 12-17 using smartphones and 4% experiencing on...

item.title: Rajya Sabha MP Kapil Sibal Files SC Petition Against Political Defections via Party Mergers
item.description: Rajya Sabha MP and Advocate Kapil Sibal has filed a writ petition in the Supreme Court challenging the interpretation of the Tenth Schedule of the Constitution, which allows legislators to avoid disqu...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Vice President Sara Duterte: Defense Challenges NBI's Assessment of Threats
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte continues as her defense team questions the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) chief, Melvin Matibag, about the validity of his assessment rega...

item.title: House fire leaves woman hospitalised, police investigate incident in Florey
item.description: A serious house fire on Hewlett Circuit in Florey, Canberra, has left a woman hospitalised, with police launching an investigation into the incident. The blaze, reported around 5:30pm on Wednesday, Ju...

item.title: Nyandarua Senator John Methu Loses 12kg During Ol Kalou By-Election Campaign
item.description: Nyandarua Senator John Methu has shared how his three-month campaign to secure the Ol Kalou parliamentary by-election for the Democratic Change Party (DCP) led to a significant weight loss of 12kg. In...

item.title: Deputy Speaker Explains ₦1 Billion Allocation for Bende Church Projects
item.description: The deputy speaker of the Nigerian House of Representatives recently clarified a controversial ₦1 billion vote allocated to the Bende Church instruments. The move sparked debates on public funding tra...

item.title: Bolton Bungalow Sells for $890,000 Below Asking Price
item.description: A three-bedroom bungalow in Bolton, Ontario, sold for $890,000—$9,000 below its asking price of $899,000. The property, located at 174 Kingsview Dr., features a 1970s-era construction with a corner lo...

item.title: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk seeks government assurance for protesters ahead of legal proceedings
item.description: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike since July 22, 2026, expressed willingness to end his fast if the government assures no action will be taken against protesters. The pr...

item.title: Radio Free Alice Explore Isolation and Cultural Confusion in New Single 'Kick In The Shins'
item.description: Radio Free Alice have released their latest single, 'Kick In The Shins', which delves into themes of isolation and confusion within contemporary culture. The track, available on Atlantic Records, is a...

item.title: Justice Carpio Asserts Philippines' Sovereign Rights Over Ayungin Shoal Amid Tensions with China
item.description: Former Supreme Court Associate Justice Antonio Carpio has asserted that China cannot legally prevent the Philippines from constructing facilities on Ayungin Shoal, emphasizing the country's exclusive ...
